signals.title: NZD/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + CB Divergence
signals.text: NZD/CHF is driving the G10 currency scorecard with a 1.337% leader-to-laggard spread. The bilateral theme is supported by a stark central bank policy divergence: the RBNZ is in a hiking cycle at 2.5% with a hawkish market bias, while the SNB remains on hold at 0.00%. This rate differential translates to a +250bp NZD/CHF carry premium. RBNZ 2.5%. Watch the next RBNZ policy decision on 2 Sep to confirm the continuation of this G10 outperformance trend.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Positioning Divergence
signals.text: USD/CAD is trading at 1.4065, down -0.62% intraday as the Canadian Dollar climbs to a four-week high following a soft US CPI report and a reversal in Trump's proposed 20% Hormuz toll. This price action triggers a severe correlation break between USD/CAD and WTI Oil, with the correlation collapsing to -0.900 against a 252-day norm of +0.001 (z = -2.02σ). Speculative positioning is heavily short CAD with COT net short at -87,846 contracts (22.6% long), while retail sentiment is heavily short USD/CAD at 73%. This extreme institutional short positioning combined with retail short bias creates a high-conviction setup for a rapid CAD short-squeeze if oil prices sustain their recovery. Watch the BoC Monetary Policy Report press conference today for any hawkish policy shifts from Governor Macklem.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD has traded to a new intraday high of 0.6979, up +0.83% on risk-on flows following the soft US CPI report and Fed Chair Warsh's testimony. The options market is pricing in significant hedging demand, with AUD/USD ATM implied volatility at 12.8% versus a 30-day realized volatility of 7.0%, generating an elevated Vol Risk Premium (VRP) of +5.8%. The 25d Risk Reversal shows a moderate put skew of -1.09, indicating that institutional options traders are actively hedging downside risk despite the spot rally. This cautious options positioning aligns with a negative economic surprise index (CESI net -10), suggesting the macro data backdrop remains weak. Watch the next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug to see if the central bank maintains its 4.35% hold stance to support the -80bp AUD carry premium.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry Support vs Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY is holding at 162.144, down -0.18% intraday following the soft US CPI print. The pair remains structurally supported by a massive +275bp carry premium (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 1.0%). However, the options market is signaling caution with a moderate 25d Risk Reversal put skew of -1.42, showing that institutional desks are paying a premium for JPY call options (USD/JPY puts) to hedge against potential BoJ intervention. This options skew persists despite a heavily crowded speculative short JPY position (COT net short -104,231 contracts). With the VIX at 16.4 (normal volatility), carry remains the dominant driver, but intervention risk rises significantly above the 160.00 level. Watch the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul for any hawkish policy shifts that is likely to trigger a rapid unwind of the carry trade.
